flop_note: the teacher runs 8 experts x 2 x 2048 x 6144 MACs per token per layer for the routed path alone; the student runs 6144 x 1024 + 1024 x 6144 = 12.58 M MACs, a large FLOP cut, but both are far under the memory roofline so bytes bind, not FLOPs
honest_scope: MoE path only, batch 1, GLM-5.2 geometry, no attention, no KV, no residual. A complete-token TPS needs a servable model, and GLM is sealed FUNCTIONAL_PARTIAL_ONLY so it is not served.

joule.reason: no accepted on-device energy source is wired; per the directive no joules are inferred from FLOPs or bytes

per_layer_teacher.note: eight routed experts plus the shared expert, three [2048,6144] BF16 matrices each

scope: the functional MoE replacement in isolation on GLM-5.2 geometry; not a complete servable model

token_roofline.binding_limit_moved_from: expert weight traffic, at 51.6 GB per token
token_roofline.binding_limit_moved_to: attention and the protected organs, at 29.9 GB per token at source precision against 0.96 GB of functional payload
